description: Static database of providers and routes the OpenReceive payment wizard can suggest for paying a BOLT11 Lightning invoice.
filter: Providers that can send to an arbitrary third-party BOLT11 Lightning invoice, including withdrawal and swap payout flows.

provider_fields.name: display name
provider_fields.lightning_docs_url: official how-to-pay/send Lightning invoice page, or null
provider_fields.icon_path: repo-local icon path used by browser UI
provider_fields.tutorials: optional ordered app-specific payment tutorial screenshots

disqualified_providers.name: Relai
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning wallet sunset — Relai is removing its Lightning wallet; users told to move funds/close channels.

disqualified_providers.name: Bitrefill
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning is only an inbound payment method for Bitrefill's own products; no pay-arbitrary-invoice feature.

disqualified_providers.name: Pocket Bitcoin
disqualified_providers.reason: Receive-only — fiat→BTC is delivered to your own Lightning wallet; cannot pay a third-party invoice.

disqualified_providers.name: RoboSats
disqualified_providers.reason: Receive-only — you receive trade proceeds to your own invoice / pay only RoboSats' own escrow invoices.

disqualified_providers.name: Lemon Cash
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning documented for receiving/deposits only; no verified flow to pay an arbitrary external invoice.

disqualified_providers.name: Peach Bitcoin
disqualified_providers.reason: P2P with on-chain multisig escrow; Lightning not live for paying arbitrary invoices.

disqualified_providers.name: Orangefren
disqualified_providers.reason: Could not verify arbitrary-invoice payout from any official documentation; excluded pending verification.
